evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long produced only 5 VALID_0DTE trades but contributed $985.6, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, 197.12 average PnL, median MFE 146.2, no slow >=12h trades, and worst MAE -346.8. Prior exit compression lowered objective and net profit, implying the edge is convex payoff that should be protected.
name: Charm Wall Breakaway Admission Geometry
risk: Small sample risk is high; loosening wall geometry may admit weaker wall-chase entries and dilute profile efficiency without increasing total PnL.
trigger: VALID_0DTE charm-wall breakaway context with require-breakout still true, but ES higher-high or distance-above-wall just outside current admission bounds.
validation_metric: Profile PnL/exposure stays >=225, total net profit is >=98% of run_001, completed trades are not starved, slow_12h_trades remains 0, and total worst MAE stays no worse than -527.

evidence: The strongest unexpressed separator remains early path behavior: bad charm-pin tails take 80+ minutes to reach first profit, while protected extended-VWAP winners resolve quickly with median MAE near -15. Static config thresholds overlap too much with protected winners.
name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
risk: A path-aware classifier can still overfit if trained on a small sample of delayed recoveries; it must preserve at least 95% of protected fast winners.
trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension entries that fail to print early positive progress after entry.
validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE from -527 toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL and >=95% protected fast-winner count.
